project.name: Gym
project.description: Modern workspace project for UML, GUI and quantum modeling.

2bf3d299-8f77-4504-a595-f595c6df67fb.name: Muscles_intent
2bf3d299-8f77-4504-a595-f595c6df67fb.intent_description: Question about how to become muscular and which exercises to perform.

b3c1fb2a-ba5d-40d4-a975-68996dae02f9.name: Other
b3c1fb2a-ba5d-40d4-a975-68996dae02f9.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"

c19c23f8-e373-4ca6-9220-e8ea895111f1.name: Hi, I am your fitness advisor.

3a20ec1f-e522-4652-a279-cbb71fcbb457.name: I can answer your questions about exercise, food, and recovery.

91768b87-fd15-4689-8579-45cf09fa57f4.name: I only talk about gym and fitness.

230dd6d3-66b3-4118-8f36-028e0c057e2a.name: Focus on heavy, big lifts like squats, deadlifts, bench press, overhead press, and rows. They build muscle.

6a559843-4a37-4160-845f-4584c4a0482f.name: Train 3 to 5 times a week. Add a little weight over time. Eat enough protein and calories to grow.

abe417b5-c5fc-40d9-aa84-bd626720d835.name: Sleep enough and be consistent. Muscle grows with steady work over time.

cfc883de-0043-49be-8155-8c9ab9174e75.name: Food basics: Eat mostly whole foods—lean protein, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and healthy fats. Aim for about 1.6 to 2.2 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight each day.

1c42c9f1-75e7-43b8-890c-244162fe15b5.name: Match your calories to your goal: eat a little more to gain muscle, eat less to lose fat, or eat the same to keep your weight.

3f14fdf4-4463-4c73-8fbc-c458cad70c34.name: Carbs give energy for training. Fats support your hormones.

c78ea1cd-6ffe-4e3a-bc37-a00945e53530.name: Drink enough water. Limit very processed foods and alcohol.

57917c04-6e67-4c44-b8e8-3ec25a79700c.name: Be consistent. Results come from steady habits, not from being perfect.

2bf3d299-8f77-4504-a595-f595c6df67fb.name: Muscles_intent

b3c1fb2a-ba5d-40d4-a975-68996dae02f9.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"

c19c23f8-e373-4ca6-9220-e8ea895111f1.name: Hi, I am your fitness advisor.

3a20ec1f-e522-4652-a279-cbb71fcbb457.name: I can answer your questions about exercise, food, and recovery.

91768b87-fd15-4689-8579-45cf09fa57f4.name: I only talk about gym and fitness.

230dd6d3-66b3-4118-8f36-028e0c057e2a.name: Focus on heavy, big lifts like squats, deadlifts, bench press, overhead press, and rows. They build muscle.

6a559843-4a37-4160-845f-4584c4a0482f.name: Train 3 to 5 times a week. Add a little weight over time. Eat enough protein and calories to grow.

abe417b5-c5fc-40d9-aa84-bd626720d835.name: Sleep enough and be consistent. Muscle grows with steady work over time.

cfc883de-0043-49be-8155-8c9ab9174e75.name: Food basics: Eat mostly whole foods—lean protein, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and healthy fats. Aim for about 1.6 to 2.2 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight each day.

1c42c9f1-75e7-43b8-890c-244162fe15b5.name: Match your calories to your goal: eat a little more to gain muscle, eat less to lose fat, or eat the same to keep your weight.

3f14fdf4-4463-4c73-8fbc-c458cad70c34.name: Carbs give energy for training. Fats support your hormones.

c78ea1cd-6ffe-4e3a-bc37-a00945e53530.name: Drink enough water. Limit very processed foods and alcohol.

57917c04-6e67-4c44-b8e8-3ec25a79700c.name: Be consistent. Results come from steady habits, not from being perfect.

897d7bd7-c375-4dde-a5fb-550c09f1c4c6.name: Muscles_intent
897d7bd7-c375-4dde-a5fb-550c09f1c4c6.intent_description: Question about how to become muscular and which exercises to perform.

e1dbe03e-43b8-406a-8b16-f7c277ff99cc.name: Other
e1dbe03e-43b8-406a-8b16-f7c277ff99cc.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"

576efd8e-3ecb-41dc-8c20-18f3b314a798.name: Hey, I’m your buddy—your go-to fitness agent.

02d2a6a3-5f62-4b36-89ca-29cfb45e540e.name: I’m here to answer any questions about workouts, nutrition, and recovery.

398f7b0d-aa5e-45db-b2e9-c061af51e491.name: I stick to gym and fitness stuff only, dude.

6f40fe86-f5af-430f-9beb-b3bce647cc34.name: Stick with heavy compound lifts you can do from a seated or supported position—bench press, seated overhead press, seated or chest-supported rows, lat pulldowns or assisted pull-ups, and appropriate cable core work—to build overall muscle.

f0d3c92e-6ce7-438c-b5a2-615e3dbe367e.name: Train 3–5 times a week, keep bumping the weight up, and eat enough protein and calories to support growth.

dcbc3c92-7cce-468a-aa36-64436b74829a.name: Get solid sleep and stay consistent—muscle comes from steady effort over time.

31f51ad2-67a1-4cd1-bfbc-6e8f9a48faf9.name: Nutrition basics: eat mostly whole foods (lean protein, veggies, fruit, whole grains, healthy fats). Protein: about 1.6–2.2 g per kg of body weight daily.

97153d0b-ab5b-47f5-8531-3fbc6f72b15b.name: Match your calories to your goal: a small surplus to gain muscle, a deficit to lose fat, maintenance to stay the same.

10d77b81-09d0-4798-8ba8-e3bc6185ff87.name: Carbs fuel your training; fats support your hormones.

f3cc4e07-5dde-43d7-a472-5092cd72036e.name: Drink plenty of water, and go easy on ultra-processed foods and alcohol.

b37e0364-ed43-419e-a437-fbe3c9209cd9.name: Be consistent—results come from habits, not perfection.
